objective: Ratify the existing canonical ID contract, add the missing taxonomy fields (layer, capability, constraints, headerStatus) to ElementData, and collapse the parallel scanner in src/semantic/orchestrator.ts so the pipeline has exactly one file walk and exactly one source of truth for element identity.

downstream_consumers: RAG indexer (will read new taxonomy fields in later phase, must not break on their presence)
graph generator (must accept new ElementData shape)

dependencies: STANDARDS/layers.json (layer enum source of truth, lives in ASSISTANT/STANDARDS)
Phase 0 ground-truth tests from WO-PIPELINE-GRAPH-GROUND-TRUTH-TESTS-001 (must continue to fail for the same reasons after Phase 1; new failures = new regressions)
public_api_hits: ElementData (consumed by pipeline, scanner, semantic, projections, RAG, graph)

risks.title: Adding required fields to ElementData breaks existing consumers
risks.mitigation: Make layer / capability / constraints optional on ElementData; only headerStatus is required (default 'missing'). Fail loudly only when a downstream consumer explicitly requests a layer-typed query.

risks.title: Collapsing src/semantic/orchestrator.ts breaks populate-coderef --semantic
risks.mitigation: Keep populate-coderef CLI surface unchanged. Re-route internally: --semantic produces the same semantic-registry.json projection, just sourced from PipelineState instead of an independent AST scan. Add a regression test that proves the projection bytes are equivalent (or explicitly diffed) before and after.

risks.title: Layer enum drifts between STANDARDS/layers.json and code
risks.mitigation: Load the enum at runtime from STANDARDS/layers.json. Do NOT duplicate the enum as a TypeScript literal union. Add a test that asserts the loaded enum matches the BNF in analyze-coderef-semantics/SKILL.md.

risks.title: headerStatus is populated speculatively before Phase 2.5 ships
risks.mitigation: In Phase 1, every element gets headerStatus='missing'. The field exists; the parser doesn't. Document this explicitly in the scanner schema so Phase 2.5 has a clear handoff.

risks.title: Removing overclaimed features (decorators/docstrings/return types/complexity) breaks consumers that already read those fields
risks.mitigation: Grep for consumers before removal. If a consumer exists, downgrade the field to optional and document it as 'not implemented' rather than deleting the type.

risks.title: Single-scanner test could pass while two scanners still run if the test only counts file walks via a fragile mock
risks.mitigation: Test by injecting a counter into the file-walk function used by both scanners. Assert exactly one invocation per pipeline run with --semantic.

decision_records.decision: Taxonomy fields (layer / capability / constraints) are file-grain, not element-grain.
decision_records.rationale: Architectural facets describe the file's role; per-element headers would multiply by every function/class/method and become unreadable. Element-grain stays for IDs and exports/imports.

decision_records.decision: headerStatus is the only required new field on ElementData; layer / capability / constraints stay optional.
decision_records.rationale: Phase 1 ships before the header parser. Requiring layer would force every element to be marked invalid until Phase 2.5. headerStatus='missing' is the explicit, queryable default.

decision_records.decision: Collapse the orchestrator seam, not the projection seam.
decision_records.rationale: WO-UNIFY-CODEREF-SEMANTIC-SYSTEMS-001 already unified projections. The remaining duplication is src/semantic/orchestrator.ts initiating its own AST walk. Closing that is the high-leverage change.

decision_records.decision: Load the layer enum from STANDARDS/layers.json at runtime rather than duplicating it in TypeScript.
decision_records.rationale: STANDARDS is the only source of truth named in the roadmap. A duplicated enum will drift the moment STANDARDS adds a layer.

test_strategy.expected_result: Phase 0 ground-truth tests still fail (same reasons). New Phase 1 tests pass. semantic-registry.json projection bytes are unchanged or diff is explicitly accepted.
